query: How many victory points do you need to win Catan?
ground_truth: You win as soon as you reach 10 victory points on your turn.

query: What happens when someone rolls a 7?
ground_truth: When a 7 is rolled, no terrain produces resources. Any player with more than 7 resource cards must discard half of them, rounded down. Then the active player moves the robber and may steal 1 resource card from a player with a settlement or city next to the robber's new hex.

query: How many cards do you get for a settlement versus a city?
ground_truth: A settlement produces 1 resource from each adjacent producing hex, while a city produces 2 resources from each adjacent producing hex.

query: What's the cost to build a road?
ground_truth: To build a road in Catan, a player must spend 1 brick resource card and 1 lumber resource card. A road is placed along the edge of a hex, connecting two adjacent intersections. Each player has a maximum of 15 roads available.

query: What do I need to pay to build a settlement?
ground_truth: A settlement costs 1 brick, 1 lumber, 1 wool, and 1 grain.

query: Can I trade with other players in the base game?
ground_truth: Yes. You may trade resources with other players, but only during your own turn.

query: How many roads does each player get total?
ground_truth: Each player has a maximum of 15 roads.

query: How much is Longest Road worth?
ground_truth: The Longest Road special card awards 2 victory points to the player who holds it. To claim the Longest Road, a player must have a continuous road of at least 5 segments that is longer than any other player's longest road. If another player builds a longer continuous road, that player takes the Longest Road card and its 2 victory points.

query: How much is Largest Army worth?
ground_truth: Largest Army is worth 2 victory points. A player must have played at least 3 knight cards to take it.

query: How many resource cards does a settlement make when its number is rolled?
ground_truth: A settlement produces 1 resource card from each adjacent hex whose number was rolled, unless that hex is blocked by the robber.

query: What's the distance rule for placing settlements?
ground_truth: You may only build a settlement on an empty intersection if all 3 adjacent intersections are unoccupied. In practice, settlements must be at least 2 roads apart.

query: Can I play a development card the same turn I buy it?
ground_truth: No. You cannot play a development card on the same turn you buy it. Victory point cards are the exception because they stay hidden until revealed and can count when you reach the winning total.

query: How does boat trade with the bank work?
ground_truth: Maritime trade lets you trade with the bank at 4 identical resources for 1 of your choice by default. If you have a harbor, you may trade at 3:1 with a generic harbor or 2:1 with the matching resource harbor.

query: When people discard from a 7, where do those resource cards go?
ground_truth: Discarded resource cards are returned to the bank.

query: During setup, what order do players place their second settlement?
ground_truth: In the second setup round, players place in reverse order. The player who placed last in round 1 places first in round 2.

query: Do you get starting resources from your second settlement in setup?
ground_truth: Yes. After placing your second settlement during setup, you collect 1 resource card from each adjacent resource hex.

query: Can the robber go back onto the desert?
ground_truth: Yes. In the 2020 Catan base game rules, the robber can be moved to any terrain hex other than its current hex, including the desert hex. When the robber is placed on the desert, it does not block any resource production since the desert produces nothing. The key restriction is that the robber must move to a different hex from where it currently sits.

query: How many development cards are in the deck?
ground_truth: There are 25 development cards total: 14 Knight cards, 5 Victory Point cards, 2 Road Building cards, 2 Year of Plenty cards, and 2 Monopoly cards.

query: Can I build stuff during somebody else's turn?
ground_truth: No. You can build only during your own turn.

query: What happens if the bank runs out of one resource type?
ground_truth: If the supply of a resource card type is exhausted, no one receives that resource until cards of that type are returned to the bank.

query: Can Longest Road get broken if another player drops a settlement in the middle of it?
ground_truth: Yes. If another player builds a settlement or city on an intersection that interrupts your continuous road, your road can be split and Longest Road can be broken.

query: If I lose Longest Road, do I lose the 2 victory points right away?
ground_truth: Yes. The 2 victory points from Longest Road belong only to the current holder, so you lose them immediately when another player takes the card or no one qualifies for it.

query: Can players trade development cards with each other?
ground_truth: No. Development cards cannot be traded between players.

query: Can I play more than one development card on my turn?
ground_truth: No. You may play only 1 development card during your turn, except that Victory Point cards are not played in the same way and simply count toward winning.

query: If I upgrade to a city, does that count like placing a new settlement for the distance rule?
ground_truth: No. A city is an upgrade that replaces your existing settlement on the same intersection, so it does not count as placing a new settlement for distance-rule purposes.

query: What if two players hit 10 points at the same time?
ground_truth: In the base game, only the player whose turn it is can win, because victory is checked when you reach 10 points on your own turn. So a true simultaneous win does not occur under normal rules.

query: Can you move the robber onto an empty hex with nobody around it?
ground_truth: Yes. The robber can be moved to any legal terrain hex other than its current hex. If there is no player with a settlement or city adjacent to that hex, no resource is stolen.

query: Can I keep building a brick road through another player's settlement?
ground_truth: No. Another player's settlement or city stops your road. You cannot build a road through an intersection occupied by another player's building.

query: What happens if every settlement spot on the board is taken?
ground_truth: If there are no legal intersections left, no one can build new settlements there. Play continues with the legal options that remain, such as roads, city upgrades, development cards, and trading, until someone wins.
forbidden_content: the game is a draw immediately

query: Can I just refuse a trade offer from another player?
ground_truth: Yes, you can refuse any domestic trade offer from another player. Player-to-player trading is entirely voluntary and optional. No player is ever forced to accept a trade proposal. The active player may propose trades during their turn, but other players are free to decline or counter-offer.

query: If I play a knight card before rolling, can I still roll for resources that turn?
ground_truth: Yes. You may play a knight card before rolling the dice. After resolving the knight (moving the robber), you then roll for resource production as normal.

query: Can I build a settlement on a harbor and use it for maritime trade the same turn?
ground_truth: Yes. Once you build a settlement on a harbor during the building phase, you may use that harbor's trade ratio for maritime trade during the same turn's trade phase, as long as trading comes after building.

query: If I play Monopoly and take all of one resource, can I immediately use those to build?
ground_truth: Yes. After playing a Monopoly card and collecting all cards of one resource type from other players, you may use those resources for building during your turn.

query: Does Largest Army count toward the 10 victory points needed to win?
ground_truth: Yes. Largest Army is worth 2 victory points and counts toward the 10 needed to win. You must have 10 points on your turn to claim victory.

query: If I roll a 7 and have exactly 7 resource cards, do I have to discard?
ground_truth: No. You only discard when you have MORE than 7 resource cards. If you have exactly 7, you keep all of them.
